_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Error Handling
summary: Handle expected failures while keeping real programming mistakes visible.
refresher: Use `try` around an operation that can fail and `catch` to respond to the resulting Error. Inspect the error, give the user a useful response, and avoid empty catch blocks that hide the cause.

blocks.body: Some failures are part of normal program behavior. A user can paste malformed JSON, a network request can fail, or required data can be missing. Error handling gives those cases a planned path instead of letting the program stop without context.

The `try` block runs normally until a statement throws. JavaScript immediately leaves that block and enters `catch`, where the error object explains what happened. Code after the failing statement inside try is skipped. A finally block, when present, runs after either outcome and is useful for cleanup.

blocks.caption: JSON.parse throws before the Loaded message, so catch provides a controlled response.
An empty catch makes the program look successful even though its work failed. Handle an expected case or preserve the error for a layer that can.

blocks.body: Trace the malformed input: parse begins, detects invalid syntax, creates a SyntaxError, and transfers control to catch. The original Error still matters during development, even when the message shown to a student is simpler.

blocks.title: Catch narrowly
blocks.body: Do not wrap a whole application in one broad try block. Keep the protected section close to the operation whose failure you understand, so unrelated programming mistakes are not mislabeled as expected input problems.

blocks.prompt: Which block runs whether try succeeds or fails?
blocks.why: A finally block runs after the try and any matching catch, making it suitable for cleanup that both paths require.

blocks.prompt: What is wrong with an empty catch block?
choices: It hides the failure and its evidence
It converts the error to text
blocks.why: The program silently continues, so neither the user nor the developer can see that save failed or why it failed.

blocks.title: Error Handling checklist
items: Protect only operations you expect can fail.
Read the Error name and message.
Give users a specific recovery path.
Rethrow errors the current layer cannot handle.
